mainEntity.name: I’ve been retired for 5 years and just got diagnosed with asbestosis; is it too late?

acceptedAnswer.text: Potentially not. For latent diseases like asbestosis, the deadline usually starts when you are diagnosed, not when you stopped working. You should consult a lawyer immediately to check the "discovery" dates for Utah or Idaho law.
acceptedAnswer.description: The statute of limitations for occupational diseases is often tied to the "date of manifestation"—when you knew or should have known your illness was work-related. In Utah, this is generally 180 days to notify the employer and one year to file a claim. In Idaho, it is typically one year from the date of disability. If you retired years ago but were just diagnosed with Mesothelioma or Silicosis today, you may still have a valid claim because the "clock" didn't start until the diagnosis. Never assume you are too late; these rules have specific exceptions for "latent" diseases that take decades to appear. Specialized legal review is required to calculate these dates accurately.
